title: Sum of Array
description: Given an integer array nums, return the sum of all elements.
solution: Initialize total = 0, loop through each element and accumulate. Return total. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Find Maximum
description: Return the largest value in a non-empty array nums.
solution: Track a running max initialized to nums[0]; update while iterating. O(n) time.

title: Reverse In-Place
description: Reverse an array in-place using O(1) extra space.
solution: Two-pointer swap: i from start, j from end, swap until i >= j. Watch the loop boundary.

title: Remove Duplicates (Sorted)
description: Given a sorted array, remove duplicates in-place and return the new length.
solution: Slow/fast pointers. Slow holds the write position; fast scans. If nums[fast] != nums[slow], slow++ and copy.

title: Two Sum
description: Given nums and target, return indices of two numbers that add to target. Assume exactly one solution.
solution: Single-pass hash map: for each num, check if target - num is already in the map. O(n) time, O(n) space.

title: Maximum Subarray Sum
description: Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum (Kadane's algorithm).
solution: Kadane: current = max(num, current + num); best = max(best, current). O(n).

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string, find the length of the longest substring with all unique characters.
solution: Sliding window + hash set. Expand right; when a repeat is found, shrink from left until unique.

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given heights representing an elevation map, compute how much water can be trapped.
solution: Two-pointer with running left_max/right_max. Move the smaller side inward, add trapped water.

title: Rotate Array
description: Rotate an array to the right by k steps in-place.
solution: Reverse entire array, reverse first k elements, reverse remaining. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Return an array where each element is the product of all other elements, without using division.
solution: Two passes: prefix products left-to-right, then suffix products right-to-left. O(n) time, O(1) extra space.

title: Fibonacci Number
description: Return the n-th Fibonacci number (F(0)=0, F(1)=1).
solution: Bottom-up: a, b = 0, 1; loop n times updating (a, b). O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You can climb 1 or 2 steps at a time. How many distinct ways to reach step n?
solution: dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]; base: dp[0]=1, dp[1]=1. Identical to Fibonacci.

title: House Robber
description: Given non-negative integers, maximize the sum you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.
solution: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). Space-optimize to two variables.

title: Min Cost Climbing Stairs
description: Pay cost[i] to step on stair i; move 1 or 2 steps. Minimum cost to reach the top.
solution: dp[i] = cost[i] + min(dp[i-1], dp[i-2]); answer = min(dp[n-1], dp[n-2]).

title: Coin Change
description: Given coins and amount, return the fewest coins needed to make amount, or -1 if impossible.
solution: dp[x] = min(dp[x - c] + 1) for each coin c <= x; dp[0] = 0; unreachable stays INF.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence of nums.
solution: O(n^2) DP: dp[i] = 1 + max(dp[j] for j<i if nums[j]<nums[i]). Patience-sort gives O(n log n).

title: 0/1 Knapsack
description: Pick items (each at most once) with weights and values to maximize value under capacity W.
solution: 2D DP over (i, w): include vs. skip. Space-optimize to 1D iterated in reverse.

title: Edit Distance
description: Compute the minimum number of insert/delete/replace operations to convert word1 to word2.
solution: dp[i][j] = min(insert, delete, replace) with careful base cases dp[0][j]=j, dp[i][0]=i.

title: Unique Paths
description: A robot on an m x n grid can only move right or down. Count the number of unique paths from top-left to bottom-right.
solution: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1]. Base: first row and first column are all 1. O(m*n).

title: Traverse a Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the values of all nodes in order.
solution: Start at head, iterate with curr = curr.next until null, collecting values. O(n).

title: Find Length of Linked List
description: Count the number of nodes in a singly linked list.
solution: Initialize count = 0, traverse from head to null incrementing count. O(n).

title: Reverse a Linked List
description: Reverse a singly linked list in-place and return the new head.
solution: Use three pointers: prev=null, curr=head, next. Iterate: next=curr.next, curr.next=prev, prev=curr, curr=next. Return prev.

title: Middle of the Linked List
description: Return the middle node of a singly linked list. If two middle nodes, return the second.
solution: Slow and fast pointer: slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2 steps. When fast reaches end, slow is at middle.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given a linked list, determine if it has a cycle.
solution: Floyd's cycle detection: slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2 steps. If they meet, cycle exists. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list.
solution: Use a dummy head. Compare both lists' current nodes, attach smaller, advance. Attach remaining tail at end. O(n+m).

title: Remove N-th Node From End
description: Remove the n-th node from the end of a linked list and return its head.
solution: Two-pointer gap technique: advance first pointer n steps, then move both until first reaches end. Delete. Use dummy node for edge cases.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Return the maximum depth (height) of a binary tree.
solution: Recursion: if null return 0, else 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)). O(n).

title: Inorder Traversal
description: Return the inorder traversal of a binary tree's nodes' values.
solution: Recursively visit left subtree, current node, right subtree. Or use an explicit stack iteratively.

title: Symmetric Tree
description: Check whether a binary tree is a mirror of itself (symmetric around its center).
solution: Write a helper isMirror(t1, t2): both null -> true, one null -> false, check t1.val==t2.val and isMirror(t1.left, t2.right) and isMirror(t1.right, t2.left).

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Return the level order traversal of a binary tree (values grouped by level).
solution: BFS with a queue. Process all nodes at current level before moving to next. Track level size. O(n).

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Determine if a binary tree is a valid BST.
solution: Recursion with min/max bounds: validate(node, low, high). Left child must be in (low, node.val), right in (node.val, high). Or do inorder and check sorted.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor
description: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a binary tree.
solution: If root is null or matches p or q, return root. Recurse left and right. If both non-null, root is LCA. Else return whichever is non-null.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize it back.
solution: Preorder traversal with null markers. Serialize: root.val + ',' + ser(left) + ser(right). Deserialize: read tokens, reconstruct recursively.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Invert a binary tree (mirror it) so that left and right children are swapped at every node.
solution: Recursion: swap left and right children, then recurse on both. Base case: null node returns null. O(n).

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just '(', ')', '{', '}', '[', ']', determine if the input string is valid.
solution: Use a stack: push opening brackets, pop on closing and check match. At end, stack must be empty. O(n).

title: Implement Queue Using Stacks
description: Implement a FIFO queue using only two stacks.
solution: Use two stacks: push to stack1. For pop/peek, if stack2 empty, transfer all from stack1 to stack2. Amortized O(1).

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
solution: Maintain a parallel stack tracking the current min. On push, push min(val, current_min). O(1) all operations.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation (postfix).
solution: Iterate tokens: numbers push to stack, operators pop two operands, compute, push result. Final stack top is answer.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given daily temperatures, return how many days you must wait for a warmer temperature. Return 0 if no future warmer day.
solution: Monotonic decreasing stack of indices. Pop when current temp > stack top's temp, compute difference. O(n).

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array of bar heights, find the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.
solution: Monotonic increasing stack. For each bar, pop while stack top >= current; compute width using index difference. O(n).

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an array and window size k, return the max element in each sliding window position.
solution: Use a deque (monotonic decreasing). Remove smaller elements from back, remove out-of-window from front. Front is always max. O(n).

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands.
solution: Iterate grid, on finding '1' do BFS/DFS to mark all connected land as visited. Increment count. O(m*n).

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference to a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph.
solution: BFS/DFS with a hash map from original node to clone. For each neighbor, clone if not already cloned, add edge.

title: BFS Shortest Path in Unweighted Graph
description: Given an unweighted graph and a source node, find the shortest path to all other nodes.
solution: Standard BFS from source. Use distance array initialized to -1. Enqueue neighbors with dist+1. O(V+E).

title: Course Schedule (Cycle Detection)
description: There are n courses with prerequisites. Determine if you can finish all courses (detect cycle in directed graph).
solution: Topological sort via Kahn's algorithm (BFS with in-degree) or DFS with coloring. If all nodes processed, no cycle.

title: Word Ladder
description: Given begin word, end word, and a dictionary, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence (change one letter at a time).
solution: BFS where each node is a word. Generate all possible one-letter transformations. Use visited set. O(n * m^2) where m is word length.

title: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
description: Given a weighted graph with non-negative edges, find the shortest path from a source to all other vertices.
solution: Priority queue (min-heap). Relax edges greedily: if dist[u] + w < dist[v], update and push. O((V+E) log V).

title: Union-Find / Connected Components
description: Given n nodes and a list of edges, find the number of connected components using Union-Find.
solution: Disjoint Set Union with path compression and union by rank. Process each edge with union. Count distinct roots. O(n * α(n)).

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array and a target, return the index of the target or -1 if not found.
solution: lo=0, hi=len-1. While lo<=hi: mid=(lo+hi)//2, compare. Adjust lo or hi. O(log n).

title: Merge Sort
description: Implement merge sort to sort an array in ascending order.
solution: Divide array in half, recursively sort each half, merge two sorted halves. O(n log n) time, O(n) space.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array. Return its index or -1.
solution: Modified binary search: determine which half is sorted, check if target is in sorted half, adjust lo/hi accordingly. O(log n).

title: Find First and Last Position
description: Given a sorted array, find the starting and ending position of a given target value.
solution: Two binary searches: one to find leftmost occurrence (go left on match), one for rightmost (go right on match). O(log n).

title: Kth Largest Element
description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
solution: Quickselect (partition-based): average O(n). Or use min-heap of size k: O(n log k).

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.
solution: Sort by start time. Iterate: if current overlaps with last merged, extend end. Else add new. O(n log n).

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description: Find the median of two sorted arrays in O(log(min(m,n))) time.
solution: Binary search on the smaller array to find the correct partition. Ensure maxLeft <= minRight on both sides.

title: Sort Colors (Dutch National Flag)
description: Given an array with values 0, 1, and 2, sort it in-place in a single pass.
solution: Three pointers: lo=0, mid=0, hi=n-1. Swap 0s to lo, 2s to hi, skip 1s. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Reverse a String
description: Write a function that reverses a string in-place (character array).
solution: Two pointers from both ends, swap characters until they meet. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string, determine if it is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases.
solution: Two pointers from both ends, skip non-alphanumeric, compare lowercased chars. O(n).

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s.
solution: Count character frequencies using a hash map or array of 26. Both must have identical counts. O(n).

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Find the longest common prefix string among an array of strings.
solution: Compare characters column by column across all strings. Stop when mismatch or end of any string. O(S) total chars.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string, return the longest palindromic substring.
solution: Expand around center for each character (and each pair). Track the longest found. O(n^2) time, O(1) space.

title: String to Integer (atoi)
description: Implement atoi which converts a string to an integer. Handle whitespace, signs, overflow, and invalid characters.
solution: Skip whitespace, handle optional sign, parse digits, clamp to INT_MIN/INT_MAX on overflow. O(n).

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given strings s and t, find the minimum window in s that contains all characters of t.
solution: Sliding window with two frequency maps. Expand right to include, shrink left to minimize. Track best window. O(n).

title: Count and Say
description: Generate the n-th term of the count-and-say sequence. Each term describes the previous term.
solution: Iteratively build each term: scan consecutive groups of digits, append count then digit. O(2^n) worst case.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array, return true if any value appears at least twice.
solution: Insert each element into a hash set. If it already exists, return true. O(n) time, O(n) space.

title: First Unique Character
description: Given a string, find the first non-repeating character and return its index.
solution: Count frequency of each char using hash map. Second pass finds first with count 1. O(n).

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings, group anagrams together.
solution: Sort each string (or use char frequency tuple) as key in a hash map. Values are groups. O(n * k log k).

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description: Given two integer arrays, return their intersection (each element appears as many times as in both).
solution: Count frequency of arr1 in hash map. For each element in arr2, if count > 0, add to result and decrement. O(n+m).

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
description: Given an array and integer k, find the total number of contiguous subarrays whose sum equals k.
solution: Prefix sum + hash map. For each prefix sum, check if (prefix - k) exists in map. Store prefix sum frequencies. O(n).

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Given an unsorted array of integers, find the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence in O(n) time.
solution: Put all elements in a hash set. For each number that has no (num-1) in set (sequence start), count consecutive. Track max. O(n).

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a Least Recently Used cache with O(1) get and put operations.
solution: Doubly linked list + hash map. Map stores key -> node. List orders by recency. Move to front on access, evict from tail.

title: Ransom Note
description: Given two strings ransomNote and magazine, return true if ransomNote can be constructed from magazine characters.
solution: Count char frequencies in magazine using hash map. For each char in ransomNote, check availability and decrement. O(n+m).

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, determine if it is a power of two using recursion.
solution: Base cases: n<=0 -> false, n==1 -> true. Recursive: if n%2 != 0 -> false, else check n/2. O(log n).

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
solution: Backtracking: track open and close counts. Add '(' if open < n, add ')' if close < open. Base: length == 2n.

title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array of unique elements, return all possible subsets (power set).
solution: Backtracking: at each index, include or exclude the element. Or iteratively double the result set. O(2^n).

title: Permutations
description: Given an array of distinct integers, return all possible permutations.
solution: Backtracking with a used/visited boolean array. At each level, try each unused element. Base: path length == n. O(n!).

title: Combination Sum
description: Given candidates and a target, find all unique combinations where the chosen numbers sum to target. Numbers can be used unlimited times.
solution: Backtracking: at each step try each candidate >= current. Subtract from remaining. Stop when remaining == 0 or < 0. Avoid duplicates by starting from current index.

title: N-Queens
description: Place n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens threaten each other. Return all solutions.
solution: Backtracking row by row. Track columns, diag1 (r-c), diag2 (r+c) with sets. Place queen if no conflict, recurse to next row.

title: Word Search
description: Given an m x n grid of characters and a word, determine if the word exists in the grid by traversing adjacent cells.
solution: DFS/backtracking from each cell. Mark visited, explore 4 directions, unmark on backtrack. O(m*n*4^L) worst case.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
description: Given a string of digits 2-9, return all possible letter combinations the number could represent (phone keypad).
solution: Backtracking with a mapping of digit to letters. At each digit, try each letter and recurse. O(4^n).

title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
description: Design a class that finds the kth largest element in a stream of numbers.
solution: Maintain a min-heap of size k. On add: push, if size > k pop smallest. Top of heap is kth largest. O(log k) per add.

title: Last Stone Weight
description: Smash the two heaviest stones together. If equal, both destroyed; else remaining = difference. Return last stone weight or 0.
solution: Max-heap. Pop two largest, push difference if non-zero. Repeat until <= 1 stone. O(n log n).

title: K Closest Points to Origin
description: Given an array of points, return the k closest points to the origin (0, 0).
solution: Max-heap of size k by distance. Push each point; if size > k, pop farthest. Or min-heap and pop k times. O(n log k).

title: Sort Characters by Frequency
description: Given a string, sort its characters in decreasing order of frequency.
solution: Count frequencies, push to max-heap by frequency, pop and build result string. O(n log k) where k = distinct chars.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array and k, return the k most frequent elements.
solution: Count frequencies with hash map. Use min-heap of size k by frequency, or bucket sort. O(n log k) or O(n).

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given tasks with a cooldown interval n, find the minimum time to execute all tasks.
solution: Max-heap by task count. Each round process up to n+1 distinct tasks (pop from heap). Add idle slots if needed. O(total_tasks).

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Design a data structure that supports adding numbers and finding the median efficiently.
solution: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half. Balance sizes. Median from tops. O(log n) add, O(1) find.

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Merge k sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list.
solution: Min-heap of size k containing one node from each list. Pop smallest, add to result, push its next node. O(N log k).
